$comment: Reference case for ggx_distribution (D = alpha^2 / (PI*(NdotH^2*(alpha^2-1)+1)^2)), the GGX/Trowbridge-Reitz NDF. Single coefficient alpha (= roughness^2). The NDF is strongly nonlinear in alpha (it appears as alpha^2 inside a squared denominator), so — like aerodynamic_drag — the canonical value equals the library default: LM starts on the answer and the test asserts it HOLDS there across the sweep rather than searching the sharp peak from a cold start.
notes: 21-point sweep over the half-vector cosine NdotH ∈ [0,1]. D peaks at NdotH=1 (≈5.09 for alpha=0.25, roughness 0.5) and is ≈0.02 at grazing — the sharp peak is what makes alpha identifiable. Canonical alpha=0.25 matches the library default; the strongly-nonlinear denominator makes a cold-start search ill-conditioned, so the case verifies the fit holds the peak shape (a formula-eval or LM-damping regression would break it).
